title: reconcile-stamp REFUSED because of dummyindex's own installed files; --force warns they will be re-reported forever
evidence:
context reconcile-stamp: REFUSED — un-reconciled work remains (advancing the anchor would silently forget it):
  unassigned new files: .claude/CLAUDE.md, .claude/commands/tokens.md, .claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/SKILL.md, ... .claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/db-specialist-agent.md.tmpl ... [then after --force] WARNING: --force advanced the anchor past un-reconciled work, but did NOT resolve it ... It WILL be re-reported next reconcile until you place/enrich it or stop tracking it
what_happened: At the end of the council run, reconcile-stamp refused to advance the anchor because ~50 'unassigned new files' existed — virtually all of them dummyindex's OWN install artifacts (.claude/CLAUDE.md, the dummyindex* skill family, equip .tmpl templates, .dummyindex_version). Claude had to use --force, and the tool itself warned the same files will keep being re-reported on every future reconcile. Claude waved it off as 'expected and harmless'.
root_cause_guess: The reconcile new-file tracker has no exclusion list for dummyindex-managed paths (.claude/skills/dummyindex*, managed CLAUDE.md, equip templates), so the tool's installer output permanently poisons its own drift/reconcile gate.

title: Stop reconcile-gate blocks session claiming `.context/ is stale` when drifted_features is empty — triggered by dummyindex's own skill-install artifact
evidence:
blockingError: "dummyindex reconcile gate: `.context/` is stale after a substantial session. ... for each drifted feature below, re-run its council enrichment (`/dummyindex --recouncil <feature>`); ... Do NOT skip silently — this is the per-session reconcile gate. New unplaced files: .claude/skills/dummyindex-update/SKILL.md."
[reconcile --json]: "drifted_features": [], "unassigned_new_files": [".claude/skills/dummyindex-update/SKILL.md"], "has_drift": true
what_happened: After the 0.25.0 skill upgrade, the Stop hook blocked the session demanding a council reconcile of 'drifted features' — but `reconcile --json` showed zero drifted features. The sole trigger was `.claude/skills/dummyindex-update/SKILL.md`, a file dummyindex's OWN `install --skill-only` had just written. The same class of false positive had already occurred earlier with equip-generated `.claude/agents/backend-*-specialist.md` files. Resolution each time was `reconcile-stamp --force` busywork. Directly led the user to disable the gate.
root_cause_guess: The gate's staleness predicate is `has_drift`, which counts any git-added file not owned by a feature — including dummyindex/equip's own `.claude/**` tooling output. dummyindex should exempt its own managed paths (`.claude/skills/dummyindex*`, generated agents) from the unassigned-files set, or at least not block when drifted_features is empty.

title: No supported way to exclude `.claude/**` (or any path) from the reconcile delta — ignore files only gate the index build
evidence: "Definitive finding: **there's no supported way to make the canonical gate ignore `.claude/**`.** ... `.dummyindexignore` / `.codeindexignore` exist — but they only gate the **index build** ... They do *not* touch the gate. The gate's `unassigned_new_files` comes from `compute_reconcile_report` → raw **`git diff` added paths**, filtered only by `_is_context_path` ... The config schema (`scope`, `mode`, `model`, `auto_refresh_hook`, `external_docs`, `auto_council`) has **no exclude/ignore field**."
what_happened: When the user asked to stop the `.claude/**` nag while keeping the gate, Claude traced the installed package source (reconcile.py, git_delta.py, config.py) and proved no exclusion mechanism exists for the reconcile path. The only remedies were: do nothing (accept recurring nags on every tooling change), disable the gate entirely via auto_council:false, or resurrect a hand-rolled filter hook the frontend repo had just retired.
root_cause_guess: Reconcile delta computation was built directly on raw git diff with only a `.context/**` filter; the ignore-file mechanism was wired only into the AST/structure walk. Config schema never gained an exclude/ignore knob, so the gate is all-or-nothing.

title: reconcile-stamp refuses on intentionally-unowned tooling files, forcing --force with a misleading 'WILL be re-reported / stop tracking it' warning
evidence:
context reconcile-stamp: anchor advanced to 303dad1bb17d
  WARNING: --force advanced the anchor past un-reconciled work, but did NOT resolve it (markers and untracked files persist). It WILL be re-reported next reconcile until you place/enrich it or stop tracking it:
  unassigned new files: .claude/agents/backend-docs-specialist.md, .claude/agents/backend-security-specialist.md
what_happened: Twice in the session (equip-generated agent files, then the dummyindex-update SKILL.md) a plain `reconcile-stamp` would not advance the anchor because of `.claude/**` files no feature can own, requiring `--force`. The warning is doubly wrong: it threatens re-reporting that does not actually happen for committed files (Claude verified has_drift:false immediately after), and it suggests a 'stop tracking it' remedy that the code provides no mechanism for (no ignore/exclude in the reconcile path).
root_cause_guess: Stamp validation treats all unassigned files as unresolved work with no 'intentionally unowned' classification, and the --force warning text was written generically without checking whether the files are committed behind the new anchor or whether a stop-tracking mechanism exists.

title: Gate UX drove auto_council flip-flop: user toggled it false then back to true within the same session
evidence:
[user]: "Option: auto_council: false corrct this"
[~30 events later, user]: "auto_council needs to be true and eveything needs to be updated and everything needs to be synced"
what_happened: After the false-positive gate block and Claude's finding that nags can't be filtered, the user chose the nuclear option (auto_council:false, committed as 7033b07), losing the drift enforcement he had explicitly asked for earlier ('update .claude to run recoucil and update .context after each run'). One caveat-list later he reversed it (committed 2e8a282). Two dedicated commits and several turns spent toggling one boolean because the gate offers no middle ground.
root_cause_guess: auto_council is the gate's only knob and it is binary; without an unassigned-file exemption or path filter, users oscillate between 'nagged about tooling files' and 'no enforcement at all'.

title: Stale retired hook generations litter the repos: frontend kept dummyindex-backbone-refresh.sh after retiring its sibling, nearly causing the backend to adopt a dead pattern
evidence: "the frontend has a dedicated `hooks/` directory with `dummyindex-backbone-refresh.sh` and `dummyindex-recouncil-on-stop.sh`" ... "=== commit 94945054 full message === chore(claude): use canonical dummyindex reconcile-gate for the stop gate — Replace the hand-rolled dummyindex-recouncil-on-stop.sh with the dummyindex-maintained `dummyindex context reconcile-gate` Stop hook"
what_happened: Asked to improve the backend .claude from the frontend's, Claude found hand-rolled stop/refresh hook scripts in the frontend (from skill v0.19.2 era), concluded they were the desired automation, and was preparing to port them — only discovering mid-task via git history that the frontend had already retired the recouncil hook in favor of the canonical gate. `dummyindex-backbone-refresh.sh` still lingered in the frontend tree. Required an AskUserQuestion to untangle.
root_cause_guess: dummyindex version upgrades and `install` re-runs don't clean up or migrate hook wiring from earlier generations, so repos accumulate mixed-generation .claude artifacts that mislead later sessions.

title: reconcile-gate Stop hook repeatedly blocks sessions over dummyindex's OWN tooling/skill files
evidence: "dummyindex reconcile gate: `.context/` is stale after a substantial session. ... Do NOT skip silently — this is the per-session reconcile gate. New unplaced files: .claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/db-specialist-agent.md.tmpl, ... .claude/skills/dummyindex-update/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/dummyindex/council/22-parallel-dispatch.md."
what_happened: The Stop-hook gate fired at least 3 times in this session with drifted_features=[] — the only "staleness" was unassigned_new_files consisting of dummyindex's own artifacts (.claude/hooks/*.sh written this session, then 11 skill files written by the 0.25.0 upgrade). No feature source had changed, so no council was warranted, yet the gate instructed running the reconcile procedure. Each time the agent had to investigate, commit the tooling files, and run `reconcile-stamp --force` to clear it. The agent's own hand-rolled gate had the same false positive in its first smoke test (blocked on a clean index because of two new hook scripts).
root_cause_guess: The gate's staleness predicate treats unassigned_new_files (including .claude/** tooling that is by convention feature-unowned, and files dummyindex itself just installed) the same as real feature drift; there is no exclusion list for self-generated/tooling paths.

title: reconcile-stamp repeatedly REFUSES over equip-generated agent files; agent forced into --force twice
evidence:
context reconcile-stamp: REFUSED — un-reconciled work remains (advancing the anchor would silently forget it):
  unassigned new files: .claude/agents/backend-docs-specialist.md, .claude/agents/backend-security-specialist.md
  Reconcile them (place files, enrich features) or re-run with --force to anchor anyway. ... WARNING: --force advanced the anchor past un-reconciled work, but did NOT resolve it (markers and untracked files persist). It WILL be re-reported next reconcile until you place/enrich it or stop tracking it
what_happened: `equip apply --for-proposal` legitimately wrote two new specialist agents into .claude/agents/ and recorded them in equipment.json. reconcile then counted them as 'unassigned new files' and reconcile-stamp refused to advance the anchor. Claude reasoned they were tooling, not feature source, and ran --force — twice in one session (planning Stop and post-build close-out), each time getting the 'WILL be re-reported next reconcile' warning. The files also kept appearing in every reconcile/rebuild output (4+ occurrences). dummyindex's own equip output poisons its own reconcile loop with no sanctioned 'stop tracking it' verb shown, training the agent to habitually --force past the integrity gate.
root_cause_guess: reconcile's unassigned-new-file detection scans the whole tree (including .claude/) and does not exclude paths already owned by .context/equipment.json; no lightweight 'ignore/acknowledge file' verb exists short of feature assignment or --force.

title: Stop-hook reconcile gate blocks a planning-only session where no source code changed
evidence: dummyindex reconcile gate: `.context/` is stale after a substantial session. ... Do NOT skip silently — this is the per-session reconcile gate. New unplaced files: .claude/agents/backend-docs-specialist.md, .claude/agents/backend-security-specialist.md. // Claude: "I didn't touch any `app/` source this session — the work was a planning proposal under `.context/proposals/...` plus `equip apply`. So the code-behavior index hasn't actually drifted"
what_happened: The /dummyindex-plan session only wrote proposal files under .context/ and ran equip apply, yet the Stop hook's reconcile gate blocked session end demanding the full council reconcile procedure. The only 'drift' was the two equip-generated agent files. Claude had to argue the gate down and force-stamp to end the session.
root_cause_guess: reconcile-gate's substantial-session heuristic (subagent count + output tokens) fires regardless of whether the session changed feature source; combined with issue #1 (equip outputs counted as unplaced files) it produces a false-positive block.

title: Reconcile gate conflates pre-existing drift with this session's drift and demands it all be reconciled now
evidence: drifted features:     assets-resources, docs-app-bootstrap, llm-uploads, mcp-server, workspace-org-core // Claude: "Reconcile reports drift in `assets-resources` + `mcp-server` (the features I actually touched) plus three I didn't (`docs-app-bootstrap`, `llm-uploads`, `workspace-org-core` — pre-existing drift from before this build)" // Gate: "for each drifted feature below, re-run its council enrichment"
what_happened: The Stop gate listed all five drifted features and instructed enrichment for each 'before this session ends', though three drifted before this session. Claude had to manually scope the recouncil to the two features the build touched and leave the rest, contradicting the gate's literal instruction.
root_cause_guess: The anchor (Model B) records one global commit, so reconcile can't attribute drift per-session; the gate message doesn't acknowledge pre-existing drift or permit scoping.

title: Gate offers no honest exit between full LLM council and --force; user dragged into the tradeoff mid-close
evidence: AskUserQuestion: "The reconcile gate keeps firing for the curated enrichment of the features this build changed. `recouncil` is the full multi-agent council pass (not a quick CLI step). How should I settle it?" options: "Force-stamp, defer enrichment" / "Run recouncil now"
what_happened: After the build, Claude did the deterministic reconcile (assign-files + rebuild --changed) and deliberately declined to stamp, but the Stop gate re-blocked. With recouncil being a heavy multi-agent pass and --force being a documented lie-to-the-anchor, Claude escalated the decision to the user. The user picked the expensive recouncil — which then STILL ended in a forced stamp because of the equipment files (issue #1).
root_cause_guess: Gate state machine recognizes only fully-reconciled or force; no 'deterministic-reconciled, enrichment deferred' acknowledgement state.

title: Force-stamp anchors to HEAD while built code is uncommitted, guaranteeing a false drift report next session
evidence: WARNING: uncommitted source changes remain outside .context/. Since the anchor is now HEAD, that source will re-surface as drift next reconcile — commit it to settle. // Claude: "the build's **production code remains uncommitted** in the working tree, exactly as the \"no commit\" choice intended. The anchor advanced to that HEAD; the uncommitted code re-surfaces as drift next session until committed (the accepted caveat)."
what_happened: The user chose 'Reconcile now, no commit'. The features were re-enriched against the working tree, but reconcile-stamp can only anchor to a commit; meanwhile the user had committed only the proposal+equipment files between turns (db2dda9). Result: the index is now enriched for code the anchor says doesn't exist yet — next session's gate will re-report already-reconciled work as drift.
root_cause_guess: Commit-anchored (Model B) semantics have no representation for 'reconciled against working tree at tree-hash X'; stamping is all-or-nothing against HEAD.

title: Reconcile delta is polluted by dummyindex's own .claude/ skill files and .context/ byproducts; the only exit is reconcile-stamp --force which warns the noise will recur forever
evidence:
context reconcile-stamp: anchor advanced to 44314747f612
  WARNING: --force advanced the anchor past un-reconciled work, but did NOT resolve it (markers and untracked files persist). It WILL be re-reported next reconcile until you place/enrich it or stop tracking it:
  unassigned new files: .claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/SKILL.md, ... .claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/db-specialist-agent.md.tmpl, ... .claude/skills/dummyindex/council/65-reconcile.md, supabase/migrations/20260610170000_asset_versions_feed_idx.sql, ...
what_happened: With scope: "repo" in .context/config.json, the reconcile delta listed 22 unassigned files that are dummyindex's OWN tooling (19 .claude/skills/** files it had just installed, .context/_enrich_plan.json) plus supabase SQL migrations no Python feature can own. There is no ignore/"stop tracking" verb, so Claude had to stamp with --force, and the CLI itself warns the same 22 files will be re-reported on every future reconcile. Tooling noise also drowned the 7 real new source files in the delta output (recurred in every reconcile --json call, 4+ times).
root_cause_guess: repo-scope indexing has no built-in exclusion for .claude/, .context/ byproducts, or non-AST file types, and reconcile offers no mechanism to mark files as intentionally unowned — the upgrade itself (which writes ~20 new skill files) guarantees drift noise after every version bump.

title: Placement verbs corrupt council bookkeeping: assign-files doesn't refresh .hash, and unassign-files re-drops the pending-enrichment marker of an already-enriched feature
evidence: And `assign-files` did **not** refresh `assets-resources/.hash` (still 06-07), yet its `spec.md` doesn't mention the version-feed I just added — so it's genuinely stale. [...later:] `awaiting_enrichment: ['workspace-org-core']` — my earlier dead-file prune re-dropped its marker (per the reconcile spec). It *was* enriched, so mark it.
what_happened: Two state-invariant breaks in one session: (1) adding files to assets-resources left its stale .hash in place, so the hash skip-signal claimed the feature was current when it wasn't; (2) pruning two dead files from workspace-org-core AFTER its enrichment re-set the awaiting_enrichment marker, requiring a manual `mark-enriched` just before stamping. Both required Claude to notice and repair by hand; either one missed would have left the index silently wrong.
root_cause_guess: assign-files/unassign-files mutate membership without recomputing or invalidating the feature's aggregate hash and unconditionally set the pending-enrichment marker, ignoring whether enrichment already happened at current content.

title: No CLI verb to write the .hash skip-signal — orchestrator had to reimplement dummyindex's hashing algorithm in raw python
evidence: Now write the `.hash` skip-signal for each (my algorithm is byte-identical to dummyindex's — it matched the MATCH cases exactly earlier), set `guideline-roles` confidence to INFERRED, and `mark-enriched` the two placed features.
what_happened: After enriching 8 features, the council-skip signal (.context/features/<id>/.hash) had to be updated so future runs skip them — but no CLI command writes it. Claude wrote all 8 hashes via an inline python sha256-aggregate reimplementation, validated only by the fact that it happened to match existing MATCH cases. It also flipped guideline-roles' confidence field to INFERRED by editing feature.json directly.
root_cause_guess: The .hash mechanism is read by the resumption logic but its write side was only ever implemented inside the council pipeline internals; reconcile docs tell the orchestrator to 'write .hash' without providing a verb, so any algorithm drift between versions silently breaks staleness detection.

title: reconcile-stamp refuses on intentionally-unowned files; only escape is --force with a warning promising perpetual re-reporting
evidence:
context reconcile-stamp: REFUSED — un-reconciled work remains (advancing the anchor would silently forget it):
  unassigned new files: .claude-design/brand-hub/illustrations/.../jsx/illustrations-recommended-step5.tsx, ... source-meta.yaml, .claude/agents/frontend-performance-specialist.md
... re-run with --force to anchor anyway.  ||  WARNING: --force advanced the anchor past un-reconciled work, but did NOT resolve it (markers and untracked files persist). It WILL be re-reported next reconcile until you place/enrich it or stop tracking it
what_happened: After committing the feature and assigning all real source/test files to features, the only remaining 'unassigned new files' were design-export artifacts (.claude-design/**) and an equip-generated agent — files that legitimately belong to no feature. There is no 'mark as intentionally unowned / ignore' verb, so the agent had to use --force, which emitted a warning that the same files will keep being re-reported. The agent rationalized this as 'the established pattern', i.e. it's a known recurring annoyance.
root_cause_guess: The reconcile model assumes every new tracked file must be owned by a feature; there is no ignore-list/unowned classification for tooling and design artifacts, forcing routine --force usage that erodes the gate's meaning.

title: dummyindex equip's own generated agent file trips dummyindex's own reconcile gate
evidence:
equip: ... write   frontend-performance-specialist  ->  .claude/agents/frontend-performance-specialist.md ... wrote 7 item(s)   ||   context reconcile: anchor 92c96bb510da
  unassigned new files: ... .claude/agents/frontend-performance-specialist.md
what_happened: `dummyindex context equip apply --for-proposal` wrote .claude/agents/frontend-performance-specialist.md; later, `dummyindex context reconcile` listed that very file as un-reconciled work, contributing to the reconcile-stamp REFUSED and the need for --force. The tool's own output is counted as drift it cannot classify. (Also note: equip printed only 5 'write' lines but reported 'wrote 7 item(s)' — inconsistent count reporting.)
root_cause_guess: Reconcile's new-file scan doesn't exclude dummyindex/equip-generated paths (.claude/agents, .claude/skills, .claude-design) even though equipment.json records exactly which files equip wrote.

title: .pending-enrichment markers persist after genuine enrichment; agent resorted to rm -f on internal state files
evidence:
?? .context/features/brand-hub/.pending-enrichment
?? .context/features/tests/.pending-enrichment  ||  Assistant: "Since I've genuinely completed the enrichment ... those markers no longer reflect reality. I'll clear them and commit the reconcile:" → `rm -f .context/features/brand-hub/.pending-enrichment .context/features/tests/.pending-enrichment` → "context reconcile: ... in sync — nothing to reconcile."
what_happened: After assign-files, reconcile flagged 'awaiting enrichment: brand-hub, tests'. The agent enriched both feature specs by hand, but the markers (and the 'awaiting enrichment' report, even post --force stamp) persisted. No CLI verb to declare enrichment complete was discovered (a `mark-enriched` verb exists in newer source but never surfaced — the agent's --help grep for assign/stamp/reconcile/scaffold wouldn't match it), so the agent deleted the marker files directly with rm -f, bypassing the safety mechanism.
root_cause_guess: mark-enriched is undiscoverable (not suggested in reconcile output, not greppable next to the other reconcile verbs, no per-subcommand --help), so the documented place→enrich→stamp loop dead-ends and agents mutate .context state files manually.

title: reconcile/rebuild output repeatedly prescribes '/dummyindex --recouncil', contradicting the commit-anchored reconcile-stamp workflow the skills teach
evidence:
context reconcile: anchor 92c96bb510da
  ... drifted features:     tests
  Run the council reconcile procedure (`/dummyindex --recouncil`).  ||  context rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster).
  drifted features: brand-hub
  enriched index preserved; run `/dummyindex --recouncil` to reconcile enrichment for the drift above.
what_happened: Five separate CLI outputs (after the feature commit, after assign-files, after --force stamp, after the polish commit, and after rebuild --changed) all told the agent to run the full council via '/dummyindex --recouncil'. The agent ignored the instruction every time and instead followed the skill-documented reconcile → assign/enrich → reconcile-stamp loop, including stamping past a 'drifted features: brand-hub' state after a style-only commit by judgment call. Dual, conflicting guidance forces the agent to decide which docs to trust.
root_cause_guess: CLI hint strings predate the commit-anchored reconcile/reconcile-stamp flow (v0.15.3+) and were never updated to point at the lighter procedure; drift messaging also doesn't distinguish style-only drift from contract drift.

title: reconcile-stamp advances anchor with no content validation; false-clean report masked real drift and recovery required hand-editing meta.json
evidence: "The procedure hinges on the `reconcile --json` delta — but my premature stamp moved the anchor to HEAD, so that report now reads tautologically clean (line 32: \"has_drift == false → nothing to do; Stop\")... are the 36 added files actually assigned to features, or did the anchor get advanced past unplaced files?" ... [TOOL_USE Edit] meta.json: "indexed_commit": "39104814..." → "a7be5dbdeb60571909e7c2ca0908bed624f13443"
what_happened: Trusting `reconcile --json` (has_drift: false), Claude ran `reconcile-stamp` and the anchor advanced to HEAD — but ~36 added + 26 modified files from merged PR #13 had never been absorbed (an earlier session had also stamped past #13, so the report was already false-clean). When /dummyindex --recouncil later exposed the real drift via `context check`, the only way to get an honest delta was to manually Edit `.context/meta.json` and roll `indexed_commit` back to the last genuinely-reconciled commit (a7be5db, PR #12). A concurrent process also committed the premature stamp (fba707a 'update indexed_commit and updated_at in meta.json'), adding confusion.
root_cause_guess: reconcile-stamp only refuses when unassigned/awaiting markers exist, and those markers come from the same anchor-based delta the stamp is rewriting — circular validation. Once an anchor is stamped past unreconciled commits, every anchor-based signal reads clean forever. No `--to <commit>`/rollback verb exists, forcing raw JSON editing.

title: Conflicting drift signals produced a tool-backed false 'no drift' all-clear to the user
evidence: Claude (turn ~215): "Confirmed — **no drift, and `.context/` is fully up to speed.** Every signal is clean... has_drift: false — 0 drifted, 0 unassigned, 0 removed, 0 awaiting enrichment" — then later (turn ~255): "context check: drift detected — 36 added, 26 modified, 0 removed  added: - app/core/ai/art_direction.py - app/core/auth/service.py - app/core/integrations/connectivity.py ..."
what_happened: User asked 'confimr there is no drift in .context and make sure eveything up to speed' three times (lines 132/134/210). Claude checked anchor-based reconcile, mtime plan-update, and the gate — all clean — and declared no drift. Only when the user escalated to /dummyindex --recouncil did `context check` (manifest hash comparison) reveal 36 added + 26 modified files and 18 source files unassigned to any feature, all from already-merged PR #13. The earlier all-clear was wrong.
root_cause_guess: dummyindex has 4 independent drift detectors (mtime report, git-anchor reconcile, manifest `context check`, per-feature `.hash`) that disagree by design — Claude's own memory note says 'Don't trust one alone.' There is no single command that cross-checks them, so an agent that consults the 'authoritative' ones reports clean while another signal holds the truth.

title: Stop-hook reconcile gate blocks session exit demanding a 14-feature recouncil after a git-only session that changed no code
evidence: Stop hook: "dummyindex reconcile gate: `.context/` is stale after a substantial session... re-run its council enrichment (`/dummyindex --recouncil <feature>`)... Do NOT skip silently... Drifted features: ai-chat-streaming, api-routers, app-infra-tools, art-direction, ... workspace-org-core." Claude: "**this drift was not caused by this session** — I only did git operations (rebase, branch cleanup, syncing development)."
what_happened: After a pure git housekeeping session (rebase, branch deletion, fast-forward), the always-on Stop reconcile-gate blocked exit and demanded council enrichment of 14 features whose 'drift' came from previously merged PRs (and was largely mtime noise from the git operations themselves). Claude had to argue with the hook across multiple turns and ask the user how to proceed instead of just finishing.
root_cause_guess: The gate keys on 'substantial session' (output tokens) + repo-wide mtime staleness, with no check of whether the session actually edited source files, so any long session in a repo with pre-existing drift gets gated.

title: mtime-based drift heuristic mass false-positives after git operations; cleared only by touch-ing every .context doc
evidence: "Both files share the **exact same mtime** (`19:48:24`) — `git pull` wrote source and `.context` docs in the same instant, and the heuristic flags equal-or-newer source as 'drift.'" Workaround executed: "find .context/features -type f -exec touch {} +\nfind .context/conventions -type f -exec touch {} +" — SessionStart reports claimed "51 source files across 13 features" then "63 source files across 14 features" drifted while git status was clean.
what_happened: SessionStart drift reports flagged 51→63 files across 13-14 features as edited-since-docs, though content was identical to the index (PRs had reconciled in-branch). git pull/rebase/checkout rewrote working-tree mtimes, and the heuristic counts equal-or-newer source as drift. The only way to clear it was a bulk `touch` of all feature/convention docs — an improvised hack the report's own wording effectively sanctions. Recurred at every SessionStart/Stop in the session.
root_cause_guess: Drift heuristic compares raw file mtimes with >= semantics; git checkouts give source and docs identical timestamps, making every pull/rebase look like repo-wide drift. Needs content hashes or git-aware comparison instead of mtime.

title: reconcile-gate keeps blocking when every content signal is clean, and violates its own documented block-ONCE behavior
evidence: "Confirmed: the gate still blocks **even though the commit-anchored reconcile is fully clean** (anchor now at HEAD, `has_drift: false`). This proves the gate is driven purely by the **mtime heuristic**, not by content." CLI help: "Prints a `decision: block` payload — blocking the session's exit ONCE... Silent (allows stop)... on the re-entrant stop" — yet hook_blocking_error fired at 14:19:00.527Z and again at 14:19:36.692Z with the identical reason.
what_happened: After Claude advanced the anchor to HEAD and `reconcile --json` reported fully clean, the gate still returned decision:block (mtime-driven). Separately, the gate blocked two consecutive Stop attempts ~36 seconds apart even though its help text promises it blocks once and is silent on the re-entrant stop. The session could only stop after the mass-touch workaround.
root_cause_guess: Gate's staleness predicate uses the mtime report, not the commit-anchored reconcile it tells the agent to run — so completing the prescribed procedure doesn't clear the gate. The block-once/re-entrant detection (likely a per-stop marker) failed or resets when the agent emits a new message between stops.

title: reconcile --json returns has_drift:true while every displayed category is zero (uncommitted removed_files)
evidence: "=== commit-anchored reconcile === has_drift: True | drifted: 0 | unassigned: 0 | awaiting: 0" — Claude: "**`has_drift: True`** is solely from `removed_files` — the **4 flow files** ... These are *uncommitted* `.context` deletions; they clear the moment the `.context` changes are committed."
what_happened: At final verification the reconcile report contradicted itself from the agent's summary view: has_drift true with 0 drifted/0 unassigned/0 awaiting, triggering another investigation round. The flag was driven by deletions of .context's own flow files (pruned as part of the reconcile itself) that can't clear until a git commit — so finishing the reconcile necessarily leaves has_drift true.
root_cause_guess: has_drift ORs in removed_files, and removal detection compares the anchor commit's tree against the working tree, so legitimate in-progress .context cleanups count as drift until committed; .context's own files probably shouldn't feed the drift flag.

title: reality-check produced 100% false-positive 'contradicted' claims: duplicate-basename misresolution, gap files, and non-source files
evidence: "_36 concrete claim(s) ... **31 verified**, **5 contradicted**_ ... - ``brand.py:429`` (plan.md) — file has 409 line(s), claim cites line 429 - ``tools/_token_formatters.py:97`` (plan.md) — file not found - ``enums.py:42`` (plan.md) — file has 12 line(s), claim cites line 42 - ``tokens.py:26`` (plan.md) — file not found - ``pyproject.toml:41`` (concerns.md) — file not found" ... assistant: "all 5 \"contradictions\" are reality-check false positives, zero authoring errors"
what_happened: After councilling the mcp-server feature, `dummyindex context reality-check` flagged 5 contradicted claims; Claude verified every one was a false positive: bare basenames (brand.py, enums.py) resolved to the wrong duplicate-basename file; tokens.py/_token_formatters.py were victims of the extraction-gap bug; pyproject.toml isn't indexed at all. After a citation fix and a re-run it still reported 4 contradicted, again all false positives (recurred twice). The report instructs "The original persona should revise or remove them on the next council pass", which would corrupt accurate docs.
root_cause_guess: reality-check resolves doc citations against the AST map by bare basename (first match wins among duplicates), only indexes Python source so config-file citations fail as 'file not found', and inherits the map's extraction gaps — no confidence scoring or path disambiguation before declaring a claim contradicted.

title: Durable checklist closes the loop with rebuild-only, never reconcile/stamp
evidence: - [ ] Pre-flight passes: `ruff check`, `ruff format --check`, `mypy`, `pyright`, `pytest`, route-count smoke; then `dummyindex context rebuild --changed`.
what_happened: The final item of the proposal's checklist — a durable artifact future sessions will execute — instructs only a deterministic `rebuild --changed` after implementation, with no `context reconcile` / `reconcile-stamp` step. Under the current product doctrine (build must end with the reconcile procedure, and the anchor only advances on reconcile-stamp) this bakes a stale workflow into the repo: executing this checklist will leave the curated taxonomy un-reconciled and the drift anchor un-advanced.
root_cause_guess: The repo's wiring is dummyindex 0.15.0, which predates the commit-anchored reconcile loop; the plan skill at that version teaches rebuild as the close-the-loop step, and nothing migrates or lints old proposals/checklists when the doctrine changes.

title: Commit-anchored reconcile model (meta.indexed_commit) breaks under rebase/squash workflows — anchor repeatedly orphaned
evidence:
=== is anchor still an ancestor of HEAD? ===
NO — orphaned by rebase … earlier: "246a6e7390f7c23af164ced06ae6dc5e01203fc0 NOT ancestor of dev" — "`246a6e73` (\"reconcile art-direction into curated index\") is **not an ancestor of `origin/development` either** — development itself carries this non-ancestor anchor"
what_happened: Three occurrences in one session: (1) origin/development's own committed anchor was not an ancestor of development (squash-merged PRs), so reconcile diffed against a phantom commit and reported other PRs' drift; (2) after Claude's rebase the anchor pointed at pre-rebase SHAs; (3) after the user's own later rebase ("i just rebased the branch") the anchor d53dc920 was again "orphaned by rebase" and had to be manually re-stamped via reconcile-stamp. Each time required manual forensic git archaeology to separate real drift from anchor breakage.
root_cause_guess: Anchor is a raw commit SHA; rebases/squash merges rewrite history so the anchor stops being an ancestor of HEAD. No tree-hash or content-based fallback, no auto-repair, and no warning from reconcile when the anchor is unreachable from HEAD.

title: SessionStart drift hook reported nonsense ('103 source files across 92 features') after the install re-cluster
evidence: "hookName": "SessionStart:resume" … "## .context/ drift report\n\n103 source files across 92 features have been edited since the matching `.context/features/<id>/` docs were last touched."
what_happened: While the repo was polluted with the install's 824 community-N skeleton dirs, a session resume fired the drift hook and it counted 92 'features' — the shattered community clusters — producing a misleading report injected into the session context (the pre-disaster report correctly said 11 files across 5 features).
root_cause_guess: The drift report enumerates every .context/features/* directory with no sanity check for empty/skeleton community-N dirs, so any re-cluster (even an accidental one) floods the hook output.

title: Drift report and reconcile-stamp permanently polluted by dummyindex's OWN generated files; no ignore mechanism exists
evidence:
context reconcile-stamp: REFUSED — un-reconciled work remains (advancing the anchor would silently forget it):
  unassigned new files: .claude-design/.../guidelines-recommended.tsx, ... .claude/CLAUDE.md, .claude/agents/frontend-reviewer.md, ... .claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/SKILL.md, ... .claude/skills/dummyindex/.dummyindex_version, .claude/skills/dummyindex/SKILL.md, .claude/skills/dummyindex/agents/architect.md, ... [55 files]
[...]
error: unknown context subcommand 'ignore'
what_happened: 55 'unassigned new files' — mostly dummyindex's own installed skill/council/agent files under .claude/skills/dummyindex*/, equip-generated agents, plus .claude-design/ inputs and docs/spikes — blocked `reconcile-stamp` and filled the SessionStart drift report wall-of-text. Claude explicitly searched the CLI for an ignore/exclude mechanism (`dummyindex context ignore --help` → exit 2, grep of all help text → nothing) and concluded 'No ignore mechanism exists — force-stamp is the sanctioned path'. The same 55 files had already been force-stamped past in a prior session and resurfaced after the rebase; `--force` is required every single cycle. Recurred across both halves of the session.
root_cause_guess: Reconcile treats every new file outside a feature's file list as un-reconciled work, with no ignore list / .dummyindexignore / config exclusion globs, and crucially does not exempt its own install footprint (.claude/skills/dummyindex**, equip outputs) from drift accounting — the tool flags itself as drift forever.

title: Commit-anchored reconcile invalidated by rebase — anchor became a dangling commit and the whole reconcile had to be redone
evidence:
=== HEAD vs anchor ===
HEAD:   92c96bb510da84e00a8d807af8591798e3a5d7fa
anchor: 52f4fa6c670a4cbf73610c58409c39c4f8238f23
[...]
=== is 52f4fa6c still in history? ===
NO — history rewritten (rebase)
what_happened: Claude completed a full reconcile cycle and stamped the anchor to HEAD (52f4fa6c). The user then rebased again and re-sent the identical prompt ('i have rebased the repo i need you to 1. update the .context...'). The stamped anchor now pointed at a dangling commit; the CLI gave no rebase warning — Claude had to manually run `git merge-base --is-ancestor` to diagnose that history was rewritten, then redo the reconcile/enrich/stamp cycle against the new equivalent commit 92c96bb5. The user's repeat of the exact same request shows the first pass's output didn't survive their workflow.
root_cause_guess: The Model-B anchor stores a raw commit SHA with no rebase detection (no ancestor check, no fallback to the equivalent rewritten commit by patch-id/message). Works only while the SHA stays reachable; after history rewrite it silently compares against a dangling object and the operator must diagnose and re-drive everything manually.

title: mark-enriched clears the marker but drift state stays fully dirty until a force-stamp — confusing multi-step choreography
evidence:
=== mark-enriched brand-hub ===
context mark-enriched: cleared pending-enrichment for brand-hub
=== re-read delta ===
awaiting_enrichment: []
drifted_features: ['app-shell', 'brain', 'brand-hub', 'shared-lib', 'shared-ui-custom']
unassigned count: 55
removed_files: []
has_drift: True
what_happened: After enriching all five drifted features and running mark-enriched, the reconcile delta still reported all five as drifted with has_drift: True — only reconcile-stamp (which then REFUSED without --force, see the unassigned-files issue) clears it. The full happy path is reconcile → enrich each feature → mark-enriched → stamp (refused) → stamp --force: five manual steps Claude had to choreograph twice in one session. A prior session had also abandoned mid-choreography (backbone rebuilt but 'left uncommitted and un-reconciled: the anchor was stuck at the old commit, brand-hub carried a .pending-enrichment marker').
root_cause_guess: Drift is defined purely as anchor-vs-HEAD file delta, so per-feature enrichment work is invisible to the drift report until the anchor moves; combined with the no-ignore issue, the only terminal state is a forced stamp. The multi-verb workflow has no single 'finish the reconcile' command and is easy to abandon half-done.

title: SessionStart/Stop hooks silently no-op when the CLI is missing, and the Stop hook is misleadingly named AUTO_REFRESH
evidence:
"command": "# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH
command -v dummyindex >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0
dummyindex context memory nudge --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0
"
what_happened: The installed hooks guard with `command -v dummyindex || exit 0` and `2>/dev/null || true`. During the window when the CLI vanished from ~/.local/bin, every drift report / memory nudge would silently disappear with no signal that the dummyindex layer was broken — Claude only discovered the missing binary because a manual reconcile failed. Separately, the hook block is labeled DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH but actually runs the session-memory nudge, which would mislead anyone auditing settings.json (and matches a project rule that NO hook may refresh/stamp).
root_cause_guess: Deliberate fail-open hook design with no degraded-mode notice ('dummyindex CLI not found — drift reporting disabled'), plus a stale comment/label left over from an earlier auto-refresh design.

title: Three contradictory drift answers in one session: rebuild says 'no feature drift', plan-update says 66 files/12 features, reconcile says 'in sync'
evidence: L90: "context rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster).\n  no feature drift detected." vs L114 (plan-update, immediately after): "66 source files across 12 features have been edited since the matching `.context/features/<id>/` docs were last touched." vs L108 (reconcile): "in sync — nothing to reconcile."
what_happened: After the upgrade, `rebuild --changed` reported zero drift, the mtime-based `plan-update` (and the SessionStart hook at L5) reported 66 files / 12 stale features (this 66-file report recurred 3 times: session start, L45, L114), and commit-anchored `reconcile` reported nothing because no anchor existed. Claude had to read council/65-reconcile.md and the package source to understand which signal was authoritative; the 12 stale features were ultimately left unresolved as an open item.
root_cause_guess: Two parallel drift mechanisms (file-mtime vs commit-anchor) plus rebuild's own hash-based check each answer 'is the index stale?' differently, and no command cross-references them or explains the disagreement.

title: Reconcile's migration advice for pre-anchor indexes is destructive: 're-run a fresh dummyindex ingest' would destroy the curated taxonomy
evidence: L108: "context reconcile: no anchor commit recorded (non-git repo, or an index built before commit-anchoring). Commit the repo and re-run a fresh `dummyindex ingest` to start tracking." — while project memory (L177) records: "dummyindex full `rebuild` re-clusters into community-N, drops curated semantic features; use `--changed` or recover via git."
what_happened: The 0.15-built index had `"indexed_commit": null` (L43). Reconcile's printed remedy was a fresh ingest — which, per the user's own prior-session memory, re-clusters and destroys curated semantic features. Claude sidestepped it by running `reconcile-stamp` instead. An agent without that memory would likely have followed the printed advice and wiped the curated index.
root_cause_guess: No non-destructive anchor-bootstrap path was designed for pre-v0.15.3 indexes; the error message defaults to ingest, contradicting the tool's own never-reclobber doctrine.

title: reconcile-stamp lets you advance the anchor to HEAD without reconciling, silently zeroing the recorded drift for 12 stale features
evidence: L113: "context reconcile-stamp: anchor advanced to a02e4370e939\n  WARNING: uncommitted source changes remain outside .context/. Since the anchor is now HEAD, that source will re-surface as drift next reconcile — commit it to settle." Then L138 reconcile --json: "\"drifted_features\": []" while plan-update (L114) still shows 12 features with stale docs.
what_happened: Claude ran `reconcile-stamp` purely to start commit-anchor tracking, with no enrichment done. The CLI accepted it (council/65-reconcile.md says the stamp belongs "at the end of *this* procedure"), and afterward the commit-anchored view claimed zero drifted features even though 12 features' spec/plan docs were known-stale. The only warning was about uncommitted files, not about stamping over unreconciled drift.
root_cause_guess: reconcile-stamp conflates 'bootstrap the anchor' with 'I finished reconciling'; it lacks a guard (or separate --bootstrap mode) that refuses or warns when known feature-doc drift exists at stamp time.

title: New 0.24 Stop reconcile-gate immediately blocks session end, demanding a ~40+ agent council reconcile mostly for drift inherited from earlier sessions
evidence: dummyindex reconcile gate: `.context/` is stale after a substantial session. Before this session ends, reconcile the drifted parts... for each drifted feature below, re-run its council enrichment (`/dummyindex --recouncil <feature>`)... Do NOT skip silently — this is the per-session reconcile gate. Drifted features: ai-chat-streaming, api-routers, app-infra-tools, artifacts-providers, assets-resources, auth-enums, docs-app-bootstrap, identity-invitations, integrations-oauth, llm-uploads, mcp-server, utils-config-fragment, workspace-org-core.
what_happened: Right after Claude installed the new 0.24 hooks during a plain tool-update task, the first Stop fired a hook_blocking_error (L800) listing 13 drifted features + 26 unplaced files. Claude assessed the demanded reconcile as '~3-4 agents apiece = 40+ agent runs', noted several features 'likely drifted from earlier work, not this session', and had to interrupt with an AskUserQuestion; the user chose 'Defer to a dedicated session'. Block-once behavior did hold (next Stop at L833 passed), but the gate's all-or-nothing demand was wildly disproportionate to the session's actual ask (a version bump).
root_cause_guess: reconcile-gate scopes its demand to total accumulated drift since the last stamp (commit-anchored), not to drift caused by the current session, and has no cheap partial/scoped-reconcile or defer verb surfaced in the block message despite a `hooks defer-check` verb existing.

title: dummyindex indexes its own installed skill files as unplaced project source, polluting drift reports and the reconcile gate
evidence: New unplaced files: .claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/db-specialist-agent.md.tmpl, .claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/docs-specialist-agent.md.tmpl, ... .claude/skills/dummyindex/council/22-parallel-dispatch.md, app/core/auth/service.py, ... — assistant: 'Several "new unplaced files" are dummyindex's **own** skill templates (`.claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/*.tmpl`, `council/22-parallel-dispatch.md`) — those are tooling, not app code, and shouldn't be force-placed into features.'
what_happened: Ten of dummyindex's own project-scope skill template files appeared as 'New files not yet in any feature' in three places: the SessionStart drift report (L4), the `rebuild --changed` output (L775, 'unassigned new files'), and the Stop reconcile-gate block (L800). Claude had to manually reason that force-placing the tool's own templates into the app's feature taxonomy would be wrong. Recurred 3x in this transcript.
root_cause_guess: The new-file/ownership scanner has no exclusion for `.claude/` (or at least dummyindex-managed paths), so the tool's own equip/council assets are treated as application source needing feature placement.

title: `context hooks install` reports 'skipped (already current)' while silently modifying .claude/settings.json (added the Stop reconcile-gate hook)
evidence:
=== refresh SessionStart/Stop/PreCompact hooks === hooks install: skipped (already current): claude/SessionStart, claude/Stop, claude/PreCompact ... === what changed in .claude so far === M .claude/settings.json ... diff: + "command": "# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH
command -v dummyindex >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0
dummyindex context reconcile-gate --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0
"
what_happened: Immediately before the call, `git status --short .claude` showed settings.json clean (L758); after `hooks install`, settings.json was modified with a brand-new reconcile-gate Stop command — yet the CLI claimed all three hook events were 'skipped (already current)'. Claude only caught the change by diffing git, then had to explain the new hook to the user post-hoc.
root_cause_guess: The 'already current' check is per hook-event (SessionStart/Stop/PreCompact exist) rather than per managed command, so adding a new command to an existing event isn't reported; output layer doesn't reflect actual writes.

title: Memory-nudge Stop hook flags a read-only audit session as needing a handoff; user pushes back
evidence: hookAdditionalContext: 'dummyindex: this session is substantial (subagents: 15; ~179,628 main-thread output tokens) and no handoff has been saved this session. In one short line, offer the user the option to checkpoint a session handoff...' — next user message: 'there were no code changes'
what_happened: After a purely read-only audit (findings written only under .context/audits/), the nudge fired on volume heuristics (subagent count + output tokens) and Claude offered /dummyindex-remember; the user's curt correction shows the offer felt off-target. Claude then agreed there was 'nothing to checkpoint' since the durable output was already on disk.
root_cause_guess: Nudge heuristic considers only session size, not whether anything changed that a future session couldn't recover from disk; no suppression when the session's artifacts are already persisted.

title: SessionStart hook labeled DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH only reports drift; one edited file fans out to 3 drift entries
evidence:
"command": "# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH
command -v dummyindex >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0
dummyindex context plan-update --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0
" → output: "4 source files across 4 features have been edited... - **auth-enums** — app/config.py - **integrations-oauth** — app/config.py - **utils-config-fragment** — app/config.py ... _Drift clears naturally: once you edit a feature doc, its mtime updates and these entries drop off._"
what_happened: The hook ran successfully (exit 0, 1.9s) but three misleading details surfaced: (a) the marker comment says AUTO_REFRESH while the behavior is report-only; (b) the subcommand is named `context plan-update` yet produces a drift report, not an update; (c) a single edit to app/config.py raised drift against 3 separate features, and clearing relies on doc mtimes ('edit a feature doc' bumps mtime) rather than content reconciliation. The drift was never addressed during the session. One occurrence (session start).
root_cause_guess: Legacy naming from when the hook was intended to auto-refresh, file-to-feature mapping at file granularity (shared config files pollute multiple features), and mtime-based drift accounting instead of the later commit-anchored model.

title: As-built feature doc still marked `confidence: INFERRED` after the feature shipped through dummyindex's own pipeline
evidence: L29, .context/features/mcp-server/spec.md: "# MCP Server — spec\n\n`confidence: INFERRED`"
what_happened: The mcp-server feature was planned via a dummyindex proposal and built (the 'WIP: MCP port' commit), yet the index's own record of it carries the low-trust INFERRED confidence marker — the same marker used for council guesses about un-curated code. Claude consequently re-verified everything against raw source (auth.py, scoping.py, enums.py, services) instead of trusting the index, doubling the grounding work. One occurrence.
root_cause_guess: At v0.15.0 there was no reconcile/mark-enriched promotion step closing the loop after a build, so docs for code built by dummyindex itself stay tagged as inferred and the index never gains authority over features it produced.

title: equip's own generated prettier PostToolUse hook reformats equip's generated agents, breaking their pristine hash baselines
evidence:
023d0731 chore(claude): keep prettier off generated .claude tooling so equip agents stay pristine
[frontend .prettierignore now contains:] 13:.claude
what_happened: Frontend git history shows the team had to add .claude to .prettierignore because the DUMMYINDEX_EQUIP PostToolUse hook (npx prettier --write on every Write/Edit) was reformatting equip-generated agent/skill .md files, flipping them from pristine to user-modified — which per `equip refresh` semantics means 'USER_MODIFIED is skipped forever'.
root_cause_guess: The generated formatter hook matches all Write|Edit paths including .claude/, and the origin-hash baseline is computed before the formatter runs, so equip's two generated artifacts fight each other unless the user manually excludes .claude from formatting.

title: Commit-anchored reconcile requires repeated manual 'advance reconcile anchor' commits, even for equip's own output
evidence:
4b212263 chore(context): advance reconcile anchor past equip specialist agents
a2b9795c chore(context): advance reconcile anchor to 4477cef6
75deddec chore(context): advance reconcile anchor to 94945054
what_happened: Three of the ten most recent frontend commits are pure anchor-advancement bookkeeping, one explicitly to move the anchor 'past equip specialist agents' — i.e. files dummyindex itself generated tripped its own drift gate and required a manual stamp commit. Recurs 3x in the visible log window.
root_cause_guess: The Model-B reconcile anchor counts every commit as potential drift, including dummyindex-generated .claude/ tooling and config-only changes; there is no exclusion for the tool's own outputs, so users pay a stamp-commit tax per equip run.

title: Drift report is ~46% noise: dummyindex's own generated tooling re-flagged as unassigned after rebase despite prior force-stamp
evidence: Drift report: "119 file(s) added since the index was last reconciled are owned by no feature... - `.claude/CLAUDE.md` - `.claude/agents/frontend-reviewer.md` ... - `.claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/SKILL.md`" ... triage: "=> left unowned: 55 Counter({'.claude (dummyindex tooling)': 45, '.claude-design (design inputs)': 7, 'docs/spikes': 3})" ... prior session memory: "50 `.claude/skills/dummyindex/**` files are intentionally unowned — anchor force-stamped past them."
what_happened: After the user rebased, the SessionStart drift hook dumped all 119 "unassigned new files" into session context — 55 of them (46%) were dummyindex's own installed skills/agents/CLAUDE.md, design inputs, and spike docs that the PREVIOUS session had already triaged as intentionally unowned and force-stamped past. The rebase reset the commit-anchored diff and brought them all back, forcing Claude to re-classify the same 55 paths again. Recurrence: second session in a row doing this triage.
root_cause_guess: Commit-anchored reconcile has no persistent ignore/exempt list — "intentionally unowned" decisions live only in the anchor position, so any history rewrite (rebase) or anchor regression resurrects them. dummyindex should at minimum auto-exclude its own managed paths (.claude/skills/dummyindex*, generated agents, CLAUDE.md) from unassigned-file reporting.

title: SessionStart drift report miscounts files and triples drift noise for a single edited file
evidence:
"## .context/ drift report

4 source files across 4 features have been edited since the matching `.context/features/<id>/` docs were last touched...
- **app-infra-tools** — app/main.py
- **auth-enums** — app/config.py
- **integrations-oauth** — app/config.py
- **utils-config-fragment** — app/config.py"
what_happened: The SessionStart hook (`# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH` running `dummyindex context plan-update`) reported '4 source files across 4 features' when only 2 distinct files changed (app/main.py and app/config.py); app/config.py alone produced three separate feature drift entries (one of them a 'utils-config-fragment' fragment-feature). The hook also swallows all errors (`2>/dev/null || true`), so any failure would be invisible. Appeared once at session start; the report was then ignored for the rest of the session.
root_cause_guess: Drift counting iterates (feature, file) pairs instead of deduplicating files, and the feature taxonomy maps one shared file (config.py) into multiple fine-grained features, amplifying drift noise; mtime-based detection plus per-pair listing makes a one-line config edit look like 4-file drift.

title: DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH Stop hook is built to fail silently — and likely no-ops on this machine due to the same PATH gap
evidence:
Stop hook command (from stop_hook_summary): "# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH
command -v dummyindex >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0
dummyindex context memory nudge --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0
" — durationMs 25, hasOutput false; same session showed `command -v dummyindex` returns empty in non-interactive shells.
what_happened: At session stop, the dummyindex memory-nudge hook ran for 25ms and produced no output. The hook's first line bails out silently if `command -v dummyindex` fails — which this very session demonstrated is the case in non-interactive shells on this machine (~/.local/bin not on PATH). Every error path is also swallowed (`2>/dev/null || true`), so the handoff-nudge feature can be silently disabled indefinitely with no signal to the user.
root_cause_guess: Hook guard depends on PATH resolution of the dummyindex binary instead of a recorded absolute path, combined with total error suppression. On machines where non-interactive PATH lacks ~/.local/bin (snap PATH churn), the nudge never fires and nothing reports it.

title: Reconcile/equip outputs chronically left uncommitted in the working tree across sessions
evidence: Next: (1) `.context/` reconcile is UNCOMMITTED (~18 files, working tree) — commit it. [...later, git status in this session:] M .claude/agents/frontend-reviewer.md | M .claude/agents/typescript-implementer.md | M .claude/agents/typescript-tester.md | M .claude/skills/frontend-verify/SKILL.md | M .context/equipment.json | ?? .claude/agents/frontend-performance-specialist.md | ?? .context/conventions/design-tooling.md | ?? .context/proposals/
what_happened: The prior session's handoff explicitly flagged ~18 uncommitted reconcile files as the top follow-up, and in this session the working tree still held modified equip-generated agents/skills, a modified `.context/equipment.json`, and untracked `.context/conventions/` + `.context/proposals/` artifacts. The user's only question this session was whether their committed work was safe on `development` — the dummyindex artifacts scattered across the tree were the part not anywhere. Recurs across at least two sessions.
root_cause_guess: Neither the reconcile procedure nor equip ends with a commit step (or even a strong prompt to commit), so index/toolkit state drifts from the repo history and accumulates as loose working-tree changes — matching the recent dummyindex docs fix 'emphasize committing index as dedicated commits'.

title: dummyindex's own generated tooling files show as 'unowned' drift, forcing --force on reconcile-stamp
evidence: The `.claude/skills/dummyindex/**` tooling files are intentionally owned by no feature; the commit anchor was force-stamped past them — they show as "unowned" in drift reports. Not a problem. (Once committed/behind the anchor they drop out of `unassigned_new_files` — a clean `reconcile-stamp` then needs no `--force`.)
what_happened: Core memory (injected at SessionStart) records that files dummyindex itself installs into the repo (.claude/skills/dummyindex/**) are flagged by its own drift report as unowned/unassigned_new_files, and the user had to force-stamp the commit anchor past them. The user wrote a permanent memory explaining 'Not a problem' so future sessions don't chase the false positive.
root_cause_guess: The drift/reconcile machinery has no exclusion list for dummyindex's own generated artifacts, so the tool reports its own installation as code drift and requires a --force escape hatch.

title: Commit-anchored reconcile broke when a rebase rewrote history, requiring a manual full reconcile/re-enrich pass
evidence: After a rebase rewrote history (old anchor `52f4fa6c` → now `92c96bb5`; new commit `d263a8bf` PR#43 "typography redesign from Jun-10 feedback", +297 lines in brand-hub) I reconciled `.context/` to the new HEAD. ... Ran `rebuild --changed` (clean, schema stayed v1) then `reconcile-stamp` → anchor=`92c96bb5`, **has_drift: False**.
what_happened: The handoff from the prior session shows the .context/ commit anchor pointed at a SHA (52f4fa6c) invalidated by a rebase, and recovering required a manual multi-step pass: re-enrich brand-hub spec.md+plan.md, touch up three other features, run rebuild --changed, then reconcile-stamp to re-anchor at 92c96bb5. Recovery worked but cost a whole session segment of manual reconciliation.
root_cause_guess: The Model-B commit anchor stores a raw SHA with no resilience to history rewrites (no reflog/merge-base fallback), so any rebase orphans the anchor and dumps the full reconcile burden on the user/agent.

title: Reconcile leaves ~18 .context files uncommitted; committing spills into a dedicated chore branch in the next session
evidence: Next: (1) `.context/` reconcile is UNCOMMITTED (~18 files, working tree) — commit it. ... "gitBranch":"chore/dummyindex-context-reconcile"
what_happened: The prior session's reconcile finished with ~18 modified .context/ files sitting uncommitted in the working tree; the handoff's top 'Next' item is to commit them, and this session is indeed on a dedicated chore/dummyindex-context-reconcile branch created to do that. The reconcile loop doesn't close itself with the documented dedicated commit, leaving curated index state at risk in a shared working tree.
root_cause_guess: The reconcile skill/procedure stops at stamping and treats committing as out-of-band, despite docs emphasizing committing the index as dedicated commits — so the final step routinely leaks across sessions.

title: Session ended with source edits never reconciled into .context/ — Stop hook only nudged about session-memory, no drift/reconcile gate fired
evidence: Stop hook stdout: "dummyindex: this session is substantial (subagents: 0; ~90,770 main-thread output tokens) and no handoff has been saved this session. In one short line, offer the user the option to checkpoint a session handoff, and only if they agree run /dummyindex-remember. Do NOT save automatically." stop_hook_summary hooks: "# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH ... dummyindex context memory nudge --root \"$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" + plugin stop-hook.sh only.
what_happened: The session changed `deriveIllustrationsModule`'s signature/behavior in module-derivation.ts, edited useBrandHubModules.ts, and added tests — making .context/features/brand-hub docs stale — then ended uncommitted with no `dummyindex context rebuild --changed`/reconcile run and no prompt to do so. The only dummyindex Stop-hook output was the handoff nudge (which the user never answered); the always-on reconcile drift gate (v0.23+) is absent from this repo's hook wiring, consistent with the stale 0.19.2/0.22.0-era install. Hook ran on every stop (3x) but produced the nudge only once (correct dedupe).
root_cause_guess: Per-repo hook wiring predates the v0.23 auto-council drift gate and was never refreshed (`dummyindex install` not re-run after CLI upgrades), so the index silently drifts after in-session code edits with no gate or nudge covering reconcile — only session-memory.

title: Generated ruff-format hook quotes $CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS as a single argument
evidence: .claude/settings.json PostToolUse hook: 'ruff format "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" 2>/dev/null || true'.
what_happened: CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS can be a space-separated list; quoting it as one argument makes ruff receive a single nonexistent path for multi-file tool calls, and the '|| true' swallows the failure, so formatting silently no-ops in that case (single-file edits work). It also runs ruff against non-Python files (errors suppressed).
root_cause_guess: Equip's hook template treats CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS as a single path; needs word-splitting or a per-path loop with a *.py filter.

title: Drift report's 'new files not yet in any feature' flags dummyindex's own generated tooling and design artifacts as unowned code
evidence: Live `dummyindex context plan-update --root .../frontend` output: 'New files not yet in any feature ... `.claude/agents/frontend-search-specialist.md` `.claude/commands/audit-devprops.md` ... `.claude/commands/paper-design.md` `.claude/reference/design-system.md` `.claude-design/brand-hub/illustrations/paper/iter-02-recommended/screenshots/01-illustrations-recommended.png`',
what_happened: The SessionStart drift hook tells every session that equip's own generated agents/commands, plus .claude-design screenshots/yaml, are 33 'files owned by no feature' that 'the reconcile procedure decides where each belongs'. This nudges sessions to fold dummyindex's tooling and binary design captures into the code feature taxonomy — noise at best, taxonomy pollution at worst.
root_cause_guess: The commit-anchored new-file scan diffs the whole tree since the anchor without excluding .claude/, .claude-design/ and other non-source tool directories from feature-ownership candidacy.

title: CLI closing command contradicts SKILL.md; Claude distrusts reconcile and never closes the loop
evidence: CLI: 'all items checked — close the loop by reconciling the new code into .context/ (council/65-reconcile.md): dummyindex context reconcile' ... Claude to user: 'the build loop suggests dummyindex context reconcile to fold the new code into .context/ feature docs via the council. I held off (it can re-run clustering) — say the word if you want it run.'
what_happened: Installed dummyindex-build SKILL.md (v0.15.0) says the closing step is rebuild --changed, but the CLI at 27/27 printed 'dummyindex context reconcile'. Claude, fearing reconcile would destructively re-cluster, declined to run it. Session ended with .context/features/* describing the pre-migration domain model after a complete domain rewrite.
root_cause_guess: Skill doc and CLI output diverged across versions; nothing reassures the agent that reconcile is non-destructive — so the documented close-the-loop step gets skipped.

title: SessionStart drift report is mtime-only and blind to the known docs-ahead-of-code split
evidence: Drift hook flagged 4 incidentally-edited files while DECISIONS.md §12 carried the real, declared docs-vs-code split as a hand-written banner.
what_happened: mtime drift flagged trivia and missed the largest known inconsistency; after the migration the same mechanism reported 'no feature drift'.
root_cause_guess: Drift = mtime comparison only; no channel for declared/semantic drift.
